title: Natural Period Cramps Relief - 10 Best Herbal Creams to Try

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols, I believe you meant to say Gingerols in Zingiber officinale, are not found, are good for period cramps because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the uterus.

Shogaols are good for period cramps because they help to relax the muscles and reduce pain by blocking the production of a chemical called prostaglandin, which causes cramping and discomfort during menstruation.

Curcumenol, commonly known as rosemary, is good for period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine muscle contractions and lead to severe cramping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a natural remedy for period cramps. It contains a chemical called gingerol, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the body. When applied to the skin, the cream works quickly to ease cramps and discomfort. Gingerol also has a calming effect on the muscles, helping to relax the uterus and reduce spasms. This can bring relief from severe cramps, bloating, and mood swings that often come with a woman's period. The anti-inflammatory properties of gingerol also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the lower abdomen. As a result, women who use ginger cream during their period often feel more comfortable and able to go about their daily activities with less discomfort. The cream is also gentle on the skin and can be used as often as needed to help manage period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream that helps with period cramps, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried rosemary leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. First, mix the dried rosemary leaves with the carrier oil in a small saucepan, and heat it gently over low heat for about 10 minutes to release the herb's active compounds. Next, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat, then slowly add the rosemary-infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before pouring it into a clean container. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil to act as a preservative and prevent spoilage. Let the cream cool and thicken completely before applying it to the skin to help ease period cramps and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for period cramps

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin (also known as Liquorice) is good for period cramps because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle spasms and ease pain in the lower abdomen.

Flavonoids, also known as rosemary, are good for period cramps because they help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the uterus, easing the severe pain and discomfort often associated with menstrual cramps.
Polyphenols are good for period cramps because they help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can ease the pain and discomfort caused by menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream has been used for a long time to help with period cramps. This is because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have period cramps, your uterus contracts and this can cause pain and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin helps to reduce this inflammation, which can help to ease the pain and make you feel better. It also has a soothing effect on your muscles, which can help to relax them and reduce the discomfort. Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can also help to reduce water retention, which is a common symptom of period cramps. This can help to reduce bloating and discomfort. Some people also use it to help with other symptoms of period cramps, such as nausea and mood swings.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream that helps with period cramps, you will need some dried rosemary leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few other basic ingredients. Start by infusing the dried rosemary leaves in the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat for about 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in short increments, stirring until it's smooth and liquid. Then, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to combine. Continue stirring until the mixture has thickened and cooled, then pour it into small containers or a larger container for storage. Once the cream has solidified, it's ready to use by massaging a small amount onto the lower abdomen or lower back to help alleviate period cramp pain and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for period cramps

Flavonoids, also known as rosemary, are good for period cramps because they help to relax the muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow, which can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora cream is a natural remedy that helps to ease period cramps. The main ingredient, Paeonia lactiflora, is a plant that contains compounds called glycosides and alkaloids. These compounds work together to relax the muscles in the uterus, which can become tight and painful during menstruation. By relaxing the muscles, the cream helps to reduce the severity of period cramps and makes them more manageable. The cream also contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the lower abdomen. When applied topically, the cream can help to soothe the skin and reduce discomfort. Some people also use Paeonia lactiflora cream to help with other symptoms of PMS, such as bloating and mood swings. The cream is often recommended for women who are looking for a natural and non-invasive way to manage their period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to help with period cramps, start by infusing dried rosemary leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 cup of dried rosemary leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Place the jar in a saucepan of water, making sure the water level is below the jar's bottom. Heat the water over low heat for 2-3 hours, allowing the rosemary to infuse its medicinal properties into the oil. After infusion, let the oil cool, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container, discarding the rosemary leaves. Next, combine the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a creamy texture. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, then mix it with the infused oil. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, you can apply the Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to the affected area to help alleviate period cramps.
remedies.image_alt: Paeonia lactiflora herbal creams for period cramps

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus cream is a natural remedy that helps with period cramps. The active ingredients in this cream, such as flavonoids and iridoids, work together to reduce the severity of cramps. They do this by relaxing the muscles in the uterus and reducing the amount of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation. This helps to ease the discomfort and pain that many women experience during their period. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease any discomfort or bloating that may occur during menstruation. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus cream has been shown to help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce the frequency and severity of period cramps. This is because it helps to balance hormone levels and promote a healthy menstrual cycle. By applying the cream to the lower abdomen, it can be absorbed directly into the skin and provide quick relief from period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream for period cramps, start by making a rosmarinus officinalis infusion. This involves placing 1/4 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a heat-proof bowl. Next, slowly pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes. After steeping, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. The liquid should now be cooled down. In another bowl, mix 1/2 cup of a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted. Then, slowly add the cooled rosmarinus officinalis infusion to the oil mixture and stir until it's well combined. Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, label the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, simply apply a small amount to the affected area and massage it in. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties in rosmarinus officinalis can help to ease period cramps and provide relief.
remedies.image_alt: Vitex agnus-castus herbal creams for period cramps

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica cream is a natural remedy that can help with period cramps. The plant contains a chemical called angelica root, which has been used for centuries to ease pain and inflammation. When applied topically as a cream, it can help relax the muscles in the uterus, reducing the severe cramping that often comes with menstruation. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the lower abdomen. Additionally, it can help ease other symptoms of PMS, such as headaches and mood swings. Some people also find that it helps to calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. As a natural and non-habit-forming alternative to pain medication, Angelica archangelica cream is worth considering for anyone looking for a more holistic approach to managing period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream for period cramps, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, dry 1 cup of fresh rosemary leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air drying them. Once dried, crush the leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, combine 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler. Melt the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally. Once the mixture is smooth, remove it from the heat and add 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary powder, 2 drops of peppermint essential oil, and 2 drops of lavender essential oil. Stir well to combine and let the mixture cool and thicken. Once thickened, transfer the mixture to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator. To use the cream, apply a small amount to your lower abdomen and massage it in before bed to help ease period cramps.
remedies.image_alt: Angelica archangelica herbal creams for period cramps

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream, also known as lavender oil, is a natural remedy that can help with period cramps. The main reason it's effective is because of its anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and ease pain in the lower abdomen, which is where period cramps usually occur. The cream also contains a chemical called linalool, which has a soothing effect on the body. It helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which can often make period cramps worse. Additionally, lavender oil has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help people relax and fall asleep more easily. This is especially helpful during periods when rest and relaxation are essential for reducing pain and discomfort. When applied topically, the cream can also help to ease muscle tension and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular choice for people looking for a natural remedy for period cramps.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream that helps with period cramps, first you need to make a rosmary infusion. Start by gathering a few fresh rosmary leaves and clean them with water. Place the rosmary leaves in a heat-proof bowl, then pour boiling water over them. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid and let it cool down. Next, mix 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler. Add the cooled rosmary infusion to the mixture and stir until it's well combined. Remove from heat and let it cool until it solidifies. Once it's solid, you can whip it with a hand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy. Transfer the cream into a container and store it in the fridge. When you need relief from period cramps, simply apply a small amount of the cream to your lower abdomen and massage it in. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosmary in the cream can help ease your cramps and discomfort, making it a natural and effective way to manage period pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is a natural remedy that can help with period cramps. The creams made from this spice contain a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the uterus, making cramps less severe. The cinnamaldehyde in Ceylon cinnamon also acts as a muscle relaxant, which can help ease cramping and discomfort. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has a compound called eugenol, which has a warming effect on the body. This can help increase blood flow to the uterus, which may help relieve cramps. When applied topically as a cream, Ceylon cinnamon can be absorbed into the skin and reach the uterus, providing relief from period cramps. Some people find that using Ceylon cinnamon creams can help reduce the severity and frequency of cramps, making it a popular natural remedy for menstrual pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) herbal cream to help with period cramps, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need fresh or dried rosemary leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and essential oil (optional). First, prepare the rosemary by either gently crushing the fresh leaves or grinding the dried ones using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler, melting the mixture over low heat. Add 2 tablespoons of crushed rosemary and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture, stirring constantly until the beeswax dissolves. Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly before adding 10 drops of vitamin E oil and 5-7 drops of rosemary essential oil (if using). Pour the mixture into a small container, let it cool and solidify completely before use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto your lower abdomen as needed to help alleviate period cramp discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with period cramps. This is because it contains a special substance called valerenic acid, which has been shown to relax muscles and reduce pain. When you have cramps, your uterine muscles are contracting and getting tight, which can be very uncomfortable. Valeriana officinalis cream works by soothing these muscles and making them less tense, which can help ease the pain. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the lower abdomen. Many people find that using Valeriana officinalis cream during their period helps to calm their cramps and feel more relaxed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream that can help with period cramps, start by infusing the dried rosemary leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 cup of dried rosemary leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the rosemary leaves. Next, melt 1/2 cup of beeswax or candelilla wax in a double boiler, then slowly add the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and creamy. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes, then add a few drops of a natural emulsifier like lecithin or glycerin to help the cream mix with water. Finally, pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely before use. This cream can be applied to the lower abdomen or lower back to help ease period cramps and discomfort.

When applied to the skin, it helps to reduce pain and swelling caused by period cramps. The cream works by blocking the production of chemicals in the body that make the uterus muscles contract, leading to cramps. Curcuma longa cream is also rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall health and wellbeing. The cream is easy to apply and can be used in combination with other treatments to help manage period pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream for period cramps, you will need dried rosemary leaves, carrier oils like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and essential oils like lavender or peppermint. First, combine 1/4 cup of dried rosemary leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the rosemary infuse its properties into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the rosemary leaves. Next, melt 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth. Then, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to prevent separation. This herbal cream can be applied topically to the lower abdomen to help ease period cramps and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a popular herb used in creams to help relieve period cramps. The main reason rosemary cream is effective is because of its anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, rosemary helps to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which is the main cause of period cramps. This relief is due to the herb's ability to block the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to pain. Rosemary also has a warming effect on the body, which can help to relax the muscles in the uterus and reduce spasms. Additionally, rosemary contains compounds that help to improve blood flow to the area, reducing congestion and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal cream to help with period cramps, start by gathering dried rosemary leaves and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine one part dried rosemary leaves with three parts carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This will allow the rosemary's active compounds to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, and discard the rosemary solids. Next, melt a small amount of beeswax in a double boiler, and then slowly add the infused rosemary oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and cools, forming a smooth, creamy texture. Transfer the herbal cream to a clean glass jar, and store it in the refrigerator to help slow down spoilage. When needed, simply scoop out a small amount and apply it to the affected area, feeling the soothing, anti-inflammatory effects of the rosemary on your period cramps.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for period cramps

intro_desc: Herbal creams can help alleviate period cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles. One of the key herbs used is 'Zingiber officinale', or ginger, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help ease pain. Ginger also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can become irritated during menstruation. Another herb used is 'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relax muscles and reduce cramping. By using these herbs in a cream, you can apply them directly to the affected area to get quick relief. Fixing the issue of period cramps is important because they can disrupt your daily life, causing you to miss work or school, and affecting your mood and energy levels. By managing period cramps, you can improve your overall well-being and feel more confident and in control.
supplementary_best: For period cramps, the best combination of herbal creams is a blend of Zingiber officinale (Ginger), Valeriana officinalis (Valerian), and Curcuma longa (Turmeric).  Ginger contains compounds that help relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which ease pain and discomfort.  Valerian root is a natural sedative that helps calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that often accompany period cramps. Its anti-spasmodic properties also help ease muscle cramps.  Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces pain and swelling. It also has antioxidant properties, which protect the body from damage caused by free radicals.  Together, these three herbs create a powerful synergy that targets period cramps from multiple angles. The ginger relaxes the muscles, the valerian calms the nervous system, and the turmeric reduces inflammation and pain. This combination provides a comprehensive and effective remedy for period cramps.
supplementary_avoid: If you have period cramps, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these is cream made from 'Hydrastis canadensis' (goldenseal). Goldenseal contains a chemical that can slow down the clotting of blood, which can be a problem if you're already bleeding heavily.  Another cream to avoid is one made from 'Ammi majus' (false bishop's weed). This herb can stimulate the uterus, which may make your cramps worse. It's not a good idea to use it when you're already experiencing period pain.  You should also be cautious with creams made from 'Echinacea purpurea'. While it's often used to boost the immune system, it can also cause allergic reactions in some people. If you're not sure how your body will react, it's best to avoid it.  'Ginkgo biloba' cream is another one to avoid. Ginkgo biloba can thin the blood, which can be a problem if you're already bleeding. It's not a good idea to use it during your period.  'Panax ginseng' cream should also be avoided. Ginseng can increase blood flow and heart rate, which may make your cramps worse. It's not a good idea to use it when you're already experiencing period pain.  Lastly, avoid creams made from 'Cassia auriculata' (autumn casserole). Cassia auriculata can cause allergic reactions and interact with other medications. It's best to avoid it altogether.
supplementary_other_remedies: Heat therapy can help ease period cramps by relaxing the muscles in the uterus. This can be done by taking a warm bath or using a heating pad on the lower abdomen. The heat increases blood flow to the area, which helps to reduce pain.  Aromatherapy, specifically using essential oils like clary sage and lavender, can also help with period cramps. These oils have a calming effect on the body, which can reduce pain and anxiety. They can be inhaled through a diffuser or applied to the skin with a carrier oil.  Some people find relief from period cramps by practicing relaxation techniques like deep breathing, meditation, or yoga. These activities help to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can ease cramp pain.  Dietary changes can also help with period cramps. Eating foods rich in magnesium, like dark leafy greens, can help to relax the muscles and reduce pain. Magnesium deficiency is common during menstruation, so consuming foods high in this mineral can be beneficial.  Finally, some people find that staying hydrated helps with period cramps. Drinking plenty of water can help to flush out toxins and reduce bloating, which can contribute to cramp pain.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience period cramps often have other ailments. One common condition is anemia, a lack of iron in the blood. This can be caused by heavy menstrual bleeding, which is common in women with period cramps. To treat anemia, medicinal herbs like iron-rich dandelion root and yellow dock can be used. These herbs contain iron that the body can absorb.  Another condition people with period cramps often experience is digestive issues, such as bloating and constipation. This is because the body releases prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to cramps. These same prostaglandins can also cause the muscles in the digestive system to contract, leading to digestive issues. To treat digestive issues, medicinal herbs like ginger and peppermint can be used. These herbs have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax the muscles in the digestive system.  Women with period cramps may also experience mood swings and anxiety. This is because the hormone changes during menstruation can affect the levels of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that regulates mood. To treat mood swings and anxiety, medicinal herbs like passionflower and chamomile can be used. These herbs have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate mood.  Women with period cramps may also experience fatigue and low energy. This is because the body uses energy to produce prostaglandins and other hormones that cause cramps. To treat fatigue and low energy, medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng can be used. These herbs have adaptogenic properties that can help the body adapt to stress and increase energy levels.  Finally, women with period cramps may also experience insomnia and restless sleep. This is because the hormone changes during menstruation can affect the body's sleep-wake cycle. To treat insomnia and restless sleep, medicinal herbs like valerian root and lavender can be used. These herbs have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns.
